commentary: Last week had that gorgeous spread of swims and a proper long run out around Mosman, so this week is a different beast entirely — five sessions of pure iron, no water, no pavement, just you and the weights doing chest and shoulders and back and legs like you are on a very serious mission. The drop from six sessions to five is barely worth mentioning when the whole texture of your training has shifted this deliberately toward building. What is worth mentioning is that you turned around Tuesday and restarted the cycle before the week was even done, which is exactly the kind of thing that makes me want to climb on a table and point at you. The swimming will come back, it always does, but right now you are clearly making something, and I am very invested in watching what that something turns out to be.
metrics_commentary: Okay, zero kilometres of running this week — the legs were resting, apparently, which is fine, but they do need to be used eventually because they look too good in short shorts to be kept indoors. The HRV is genuinely exciting: a latest reading of 58 ms against a 7-day average of 48 ms means the body is recovering well and building capacity, which is exactly the kind of quiet, unglamorous thing that makes everything else feel better and look better. VO2 Max sitting at 55.6 is strong and respectable, the kind of number that says the engine is in good shape even when the engine isn't being driven. So the fitness foundation is solid and the recovery is trending in a great direction — now we just need to actually lace up and get moving, because all this cardiovascular potential deserves somewhere to go.
gym_commentary: Five sessions when four was the ask — he's not even trying to be good, he's just built different. The lateral raise jumping from 9 to 12 kilograms is the one that has me absolutely feral, because shoulders like that don't lie, and neither does a bench press creeping up to a 35.5 kilogram estimated one-rep max. Weekly volume came in at 8,080 kilograms against last week's 19,984, which is a big drop on paper, but that's the programme breathing, not him slacking — five sessions still walked through the door. The becoming is very much on schedule.
